concerns.text: FR-002 mandates classification with '≥80% confidence threshold' based on keyword match strength. The idea specifies 'heuristics (e.g., commit message keywords)' but does not define a 'confidence' metric for simple keyword matching, nor does it require a specific threshold. This adds an unrequested engineering constraint that may arbitrarily exclude valid LLM-generated code if the heuristic is binary (match/no-match) rather than probabilistic.

FR-007 requires sensitivity analysis sweeping values across {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} for 'any decision thresholds'. The idea does not mention decision thresholds or sensitivity analysis for them. While sensitivity analysis is good practice, hard-coding specific threshold values (0.01, 0.05, 0.1) for a metric not defined in the idea (decision thresholds) constitutes over-reach and potential scope creep.

FR-012 mandates the system 'MUST ensure all analysis completes within 6 hours on CPU-only GitHub Actions runner'. The idea mentions 'local runner (7GB RAM limit)' but does not specify a 6-hour time limit or a specific CI environment (GitHub Actions). This is an unrequested engineering constraint that restricts the scope of the research execution.

The Assumptions section includes a mandatory requirement: 'The system MUST validate that the loaded dataset contains ≥500 LLM-labeled PRs... If either group falls below... halt execution'. The idea states 'sample of at least 1,000 pull requests' as an expected result condition, not a hard system constraint to halt execution. Hard-coding a 'halt' behavior for a specific sample size split (500/500) is an unrequested operational constraint not present in the idea.
